commit.message:
feat: add SPLIT_ROUTING toggle — route all traffic through proxy (#29)

Adds a `SPLIT_ROUTING` env var (default `true`) to disable geo-based
split routing entirely, sending all traffic through the proxy outbound.

## Changes

- **`config_generator.py`** — `ConfigSettings` gets `split_route: bool =
True`; when `False`, `_build_dns` emits no direct-site DNS rules and
`_build_route` drops all geo/custom direct rules and `rule_set` entries
(only `sniff`, `hijack-dns`, and private-IP-direct remain)
- **`sync.py`** — reads `SPLIT_ROUTING` env var, passes `split_route` to
`ConfigSettings`; skips `ensure_rule_sets` entirely when split routing
is off
- **`tui.py`** — `SPLIT_ROUTING` added to `EDITABLE_KEYS` in
`ConfigScreen`; `NodesScreen` node-switch respects the setting
- **`config.env.example`** — documents `SPLIT_ROUTING=true` (commented
out)

Set `SPLIT_ROUTING=false` (also accepts `0` / `no`) to proxy all
traffic.

[analysis] Paper 8: Kac identifiability scaffold — inverse solver, tests, LaTeX (#198)

Scaffold for Paper 8: 'Can You Hear the Shape of an Organ?'

New module src/analytical/kac_identifiability.py:
- compute_jacobian() with scaled/raw modes
- jacobian_condition_number() using dimensionless sensitivity matrix
- condition_number_map() for parameter-space sweeps
- invert_frequencies() with normalised Newton-Raphson solver

- Sphere scaled condition number: ~1.1e10 (rank deficient)
- Oblate Ritz scaled condition number: ~73 (full rank, well-conditioned)

commit.message:
feat: custom geo rule set URLs for direct routing (RULE_SET_DIRECT_IP_URLS / RULE_SET_DIRECT_SITE_URLS) (#28)

Extends geo rule set support beyond SagerNet's sing-geoip/sing-geosite
to allow arbitrary `.srs` sources (e.g. AntifilterCommunity for RKN
bypass, XTLS/geoip, Loyalsoldier/sing-box-rules).

## What and why

Two new env vars accept `tag:https://url,...` pairs. On sync, files are
downloaded to `RULE_SET_DIR/{tag}.srs` if absent, then wired into
sing-box config as `local` rule sets:

- `RULE_SET_DIRECT_IP_URLS` — IP-based: routing only
- `RULE_SET_DIRECT_SITE_URLS` — domain-based: DNS (route → local) +
routing

```bash
# config.env — bypass RKN-blocked domains/IPs without proxying
RULE_SET_DIRECT_SITE_URLS=antifilter-community:https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AntifilterCommunity/antifilter-ruleset/main/antifilter-community.srs
RULE_SET_DIRECT_IP_URLS=antifilter-ip-community:https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AntifilterCommunity/antifilter-ruleset/main/antifilter-ip-community.srs
```

**Changes:**
- `config_generator.py` — `ConfigSettings` gains
`custom_direct_ip_rule_sets` / `custom_direct_site_rule_sets`
(`List[Tuple[str, str]]`); `_build_route` and `_build_dns` emit entries
for them
- `binary_manager.py` — `ensure_rule_sets` accepts and downloads custom
rule set URL pairs
- `sync.py` — `_parse_rule_set_urls` parses the env vars;
`ConfigSettings` and `ensure_rule_sets` calls updated
- `config.env.example` / `README.md` — documented with
AntifilterCommunity example URLs

Fully backward-compatible; existing `GEO_DIRECT_IP`/`GEO_DIRECT_SITE`
behaviour unchanged.

## Type
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Docs / cleanup

- [ ] Tested on actual VyOS (or clearly noted as untested)

commit.message:
perf: O(1) ID index for <use>, eliminate AffineTransform allocations in skew/matrix transforms

Closes #140 (partially), addresses #142.

Changes:
- Surface: add nodeById Map<String,Node> populated during parseAllDefs for O(1)
  element lookup instead of O(n) recursive tree walk on every <use> render
- Defs.parseDef: index all nodes with an id into surface.nodeById
- Defs.use: replace findNodeById() tree traversal with surface.nodeById.get()
- Surface.draw: remove redundant AffineTransform savedTransform allocation
  (context.getTransform() was called twice; savedTransform was never read back)
- Helpers.parseTransform: replace skewX/skewY AffineTransform allocations with
  matrix.shear() which is equivalent but allocation-free; reuse a thread-local
  AffineTransform for the matrix() transform function

fix: sing-box binary auto-download on container start/update and install (#27)

`binary_manager.ensure_sing_box/ensure_rule_sets` were defined but never
called — `sync.py` only validated binary presence and hard-failed. On a
fresh container or after an image update (binary not in any volume),
this caused an unhandled `FileNotFoundError` that silently killed the
sing-box thread.

## What and why

**Root causes:**
- `sync.py` checked for binary existence but never downloaded it;
`binary_manager.py` was dead code
- Binary/ruleset download happened *after* the `SUBSCRIPTION_URL` guard,
so a fresh install with unconfigured URL never got the binary either
- `Popen` raising `OSError` in `_run_sing_box` bypassed the restart
counter entirely, leaving the daemon running with no sing-box and no
stop signal
- `install.sh` would abort via `set -e` if first sync failed (e.g.
`SUBSCRIPTION_URL` not yet set), skipping service enablement
- `sing-box.service` hardcoded `/usr/local/bin/sing-box` instead of
reading `XRAY_BIN` from the env file

**Changes:**

- **`src/sync.py`** — wire in `ensure_sing_box()` +
`ensure_rule_sets()`; move both calls *before* the `SUBSCRIPTION_URL`
check so the binary is always fetched regardless of subscription state
- **`src/daemon.py`** — wrap `subprocess.Popen` in `try/except OSError`;
launch failures now log, set `ret = 127`, and feed into the existing
restart/stop logic
- **`install.sh`** — make initial sync soft-fail (`|| echo ...`); binary
is downloaded as a side-effect even when URL isn't configured yet, and
services are always enabled
- **`systemd/sing-box.service`** — add
`EnvironmentFile=-/etc/remnaproxy/config.env` with fallback
`Environment=` defaults; use `$XRAY_BIN`/`$XRAY_CONFIG` in `Exec*` lines
- **`config.env.example`** — add comment that `XRAY_BIN` is
auto-downloaded on first sync if missing

## Type
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Docs / cleanup

## Checklist
- [x] `python -m pytest tests/ -v` passes
- [ ] Tested on actual VyOS (or clearly noted as untested)
- [x] No hardcoded paths / credentials

commit.message:
fix: IAM propagation delay on CodeBuild project creation

When SDK deletes and recreates the CodeBuild execution role between
failed runs, CreateProject immediately fails because IAM takes 10-30s
to propagate trust policies.

Fixes:
- patch_agentcore_yaml(): verify each stored ARN actually exists in AWS
  via 'aws iam get-role' / 'aws ecr describe-repositories' before trusting
  it. Stale ARNs (role was deleted) are set to null so SDK auto-creates
  fresh resources instead of using a ghost reference.
- launch_agentcore(): retry loop (3 attempts, 30s wait between) that
  detects IAM trust policy errors in the output and retries. Also
  regenerates the YAML before each retry (SDK deletes it on failure).

commit.message:
perf: avoid caching sub-region maskBuffer; add sub-region mask test

When paintMask() is called for a sub-region effect (subRegionTransform != null),
the mask buffer is small (element-bbox-sized) and should not be saved to
surface.maskBuffer. Storing a small sub-region buffer would evict the reusable
full-canvas buffer, forcing re-allocation on the next full-canvas mask call.

Fix mirrors the effectBuffer pattern in Surface.draw():
- Full-canvas masks (subRegionTransform == null): reuse or allocate and cache
- Sub-region masks (subRegionTransform != null): allocate a temporary buffer, skip cache

Also adds testSubRegionMaskCorrectness() to ShapeRenderingTest to verify that:
- Pixels inside a small masked element on a large canvas have correct alpha
- Pixels outside the element bbox remain transparent

Closes #154

fix: self-healing YAML regeneration + restore deleted config

The SDK deletes .bedrock_agentcore.yaml on failed runs, breaking
subsequent deploys with FileNotFoundError.

Fix: rewrite patch_agentcore_yaml() to always regenerate the YAML
from scratch using Python yaml.dump, pulling values from:

  2. Existing YAML (if present) — preserves SDK-populated ARNs
  3. Script variables (AGENTCORE_REGION, ACCOUNT_ID, AGENT_NAME)

whether the SDK deleted or corrupted it in a previous run.

commit.message:
refactor: feed-rate based multi-pass playback with M0 pause

- Replace flat playSeq/playIdx with multi-pass state: playPasses[],
  playPassIdx, playSegIdx, playDistMm — one pass per contour group and
  per shade band
- Precompute lenMm per segment; playback advances via a 1/60s time
  budget consumed at feedRate×speedMult (draws) and 5× that (travels),
  giving realistic speed proportional to the G-code settings feed rate
- Speed slider is now a multiplier (0.25×–8×, default 1×) shown inline
- After each pass completes, playPausedM0 = true; UI shows M0 indicator
  with next pass label and a Continue button; onResumeM0() advances to
  the next pass — mirrors the physical pen-swap workflow
- Completed passes are drawn fully in their colors; current pass draws
  progressively with sub-segment interpolation for smooth pen dot
- Removed the static shading-pass background rendered during playback
  (shade passes now animate in order instead)
- shadingPasses store change now resets playback state

commit.message:
fix: redirect display functions to stderr; remove reserved AWS_REGION Lambda env var

Two bugs fixed:

1. header/step/ok/warn wrote to stdout, so any function whose return value
   was captured with $() (e.g. deploy_lambda, get_or_create_deploy_id) would
   mix progress messages into the captured value. The CLOUDTRAIL_LAMBDA_ARN
   and DYNAMODB_LAMBDA_ARN variables were getting the full 3-line progress
   output prepended to the ARN, making the EventBridge --targets JSON invalid.
   Fix: all display functions now write to stderr (>&2). Only data intended as
   a return value is written to stdout.

2. AWS_REGION is a Lambda-reserved environment variable; it is injected
   automatically by the Lambda runtime and cannot be overridden in function
   configuration. Removed it from both Lambda env var strings. The Python
   code already reads os.environ.get('AWS_REGION', 'eu-west-2') which will
   pick up the runtime-provided value correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
